seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Semantic Kernel enables powerful code-aware agents that analyze repositories, generate fixes, and validate through sandbox execution. However, plugin chaining, planners, and memory systems introduce risks like cascading errors, invalid plans, and data contamination that can produce insecure or incorrect code. Vex integration as a kernel filter provides essential guardrails for code correctness, security, and compliance in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Coding Assistant uses Semantic Kernel's plugin system to orchestrate repository analysis, code generation, sandbox execution, and test validation. A central planner decomposes user requests into plugin execution sequences, leveraging memory stores for conversation history and repository context. Native plugins handle git operations, AST parsing, sandboxed Python/Node execution, and pytest/Jest validation, with iterative debugging loops refining outputs.

Kernel functions are chained through planners (Handlebars or Sequential), with memory recall providing codebase context and chat history. Results flow through verification plugins before final delivery, ensuring code safety and correctness. Vex integrates as a post-function filter to catch failures across this pipeline.

This architecture scales to enterprise needs but amplifies risks when planners generate invalid sequences or memory hallucinates context, making runtime verification essential.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Repo analysis plugin fails silently, causing AST parser to receive malformed data and generate invalid fixes
risks.mitigation: Vex result filters validate each plugin output before chaining to next function

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Handlebars planner creates non-existent plugin sequences, leading to runtime exceptions during code generation
risks.mitigation: Vex plan validation middleware intercepts and corrects malformed execution graphs

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: VolatileVectorStore retrieves fabricated commit history, causing assistant to reference non-existent code changes
risks.mitigation: Vex memory verification ensures recalled context matches actual repository state

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: Security scanner plugin variables leak into test executor, exposing sandbox to malicious payloads
risks.mitigation: Vex data isolation policies sanitize inputs between plugin invocations

implementation.title: Repository Context Plugin
implementation.description: Semantic Kernel plugin that clones repo, extracts relevant files, and builds context for analysis

implementation.title: Sandbox Code Executor
implementation.description: Isolated Python execution plugin with timeout and permission restrictions

implementation.title: TypeScript Test Validator
implementation.description: Jest test runner plugin for Node.js code validation

implementation.title: Iterative Debug Planner
implementation.description: Handlebars planner that orchestrates analysis-fixes-validation-debug loop
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel.planners import HandlebarsPlanner

planner = HandlebarsPlanner(kernel)

async def create_debug_plan(user_request: str, repo_url: str) -> str:
    plan = await planner.create_plan(
        goal=f"""
        # AI Coding Assistant Debug Workflow
        1. Get repo context from {repo_url}
        2. Analyze code issues in user request: {user_request}
        3. Generate fix using analysis
        4. Execute fix in sandbox
        5. Run tests on fixed code
        6. If tests fail, iterate with test feedback
        7. Return final verified code
        """
    )
    return plan
